# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and maintainers of BalatroEditor pledge to make participation in our project and community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity, level of experience, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity, or the tools and technologies they use (including AI assistants, code generators, or any development tooling).

## Our Standards

### Expected Behavior

- **Be respectful and empathetic.** Disagreement is natural — approach it constructively.
- **Critique ideas, not people.** Focus on what can be improved, not who made the mistake.
- **Assume good intent.** Ask clarifying questions before concluding someone is wrong.
- **Welcome newcomers.** Everyone starts somewhere. Patience and kindness cost nothing.
- **Give and accept feedback gracefully.** Feedback is about the work, not the person.

### Unacceptable Behavior

- **Personal attacks, insults, or derogatory comments.**
- **Judging or shaming others** for their skill level, experience, questions, mistakes, or the tools they use. This includes, but is not limited to:
  - Mocking someone for using AI, not using AI, or using a particular framework or approach.
  - Dismissing contributions because they don't meet an arbitrary "real developer" standard.
  - Gatekeeping based on experience or tooling choices.
- **Demonizing AI or AI-assisted development.** Whether you use AI or not, shaming others for their choice is not acceptable. AI is a tool — how people use it is their own professional judgment.
- **Harassment of any kind** — public or private.
- **Publishing others' private information** without explicit consent.
- **Sexualized content or language** of any kind.
- **Trolling, baiting, or intentionally inflammatory comments.**

## Enforcement

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing this Code of Conduct. They have the right to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that violate this policy, and may temporarily or permanently ban any contributor for behaviors they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

### Reporting

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, report it by opening an issue or contacting the project maintainer directly. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all project spaces — including GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, and any other project-related channels — and also applies when an individual is representing the project in public spaces.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1, with modifications to explicitly address AI-tooling respect and judgment-free collaboration.
